page: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX2, AES, and SHA_NI.

This server is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include bundled local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach high-performance external block storage. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and single-threaded compression and decompression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average range. In cryptographic and extended instruction tests, the processor demonstrates strong performance in the top 25%. LLM inference benchmarks show strong performance for smaller models but drop to poor levels for large models due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the E64-32as_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines. Workloads requiring heavy multi-threaded raw compute or local high-speed scratch space may face tradeoffs due to average multi-core scaling and the lack of local SSDs.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating at 4.5 GHz, this x86_64 server provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores and features a substantial 512.0 GB of RAM, translating to 16.0 GB per core. While it lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong cryptographic capabilities. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain average. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as large databases, enterprise applications, and in-memory caching, where high RAM capacity is critical and external storage is utilized.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC processor, 512 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, built for memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine delivering high RAM capacity and top-tier single-core performance for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
High-capacity 512.0 GB of RAM providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Best suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
